key_insight: A hybrid UNet-Transformer can act as a fast surrogate for FEM by mapping composite microstructure geometry directly to stress and strain field images with high accuracy across multiple loading and tessellation settings.
engineering_relevance: This enables rapid mechanical field prediction for composite design studies, reducing dependence on expensive finite element simulations while preserving physically meaningful stress localization.
method: Inputs: composite microstructure geometry images from two-phase hierarchical composites.
Model: UNet encoder-decoder with Transformer components to capture both local detail and long-range mechanical context.
Outputs: predicted stress/strain field maps across ten field types and varied boundary conditions.
evidence_signal: The model achieved strong results on most subdatasets, including R2=0.9991 and SSIM=0.9936 on periodic tessellation cases, with MAE below 0.05 on six of eight evaluated subdatasets.
watch_item: Performance drops on irregular square-grid geometries with sparse soft-phase inclusions, indicating difficulty reproducing sharp stress discontinuities in challenging configurations.
paper_title: Hybrid Unet-Transformer Model for Generating Stress and Strain Fields from Composite Geometrics
